items.title: USDA sets SNAP FY2026 maximum allotments (Oct 2025–Sept 2026): $298 for 1 person, $994 for 4 in the 48 states/DC
items.summary: USDA Food and Nutrition Service published FY2026 SNAP standards effective Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026. The maximum monthly allotment in the 48 states and D.C. is $298 for a household of 1, $546 for 2, $785 for 3, and $994 for 4; standard deductions range from $209 (1–3 people) to $299 (6+).

items.title: USDA lists new SNAP food-restriction waivers with 2026 target dates (e.g., Arkansas 7/1/26; South Carolina 8/31/26)
items.summary: USDA FNS updated its tracker of approved SNAP food-restriction waivers showing multiple states with 2026 target implementation dates. Examples include Arkansas targeting July 1, 2026 to restrict soda and candy (and certain drinks with less than 50% juice) and South Carolina targeting Aug. 31, 2026 to restrict candy, energy drinks, and sweetened beverages.

items.title: CMS issues revised Medicaid managed care guidance limiting certain state directed payments tied to Medicare rates (effective for rating periods starting on/after July 4, 2025)
items.summary: CMS issued revised guidance implementing Section 71116 of Public Law 119-21 that reduces the total payment rate limit for certain Medicaid managed care state directed payments (SDPs), including inpatient/outpatient hospital, nursing facility, and certain academic medical center practitioner services. The guidance describes limits of 100% of published Medicare rates for expansion states and 110% for non-expansion states, with temporary grandfathering for specified SDPs until rating periods beginning on/after Jan. 1, 2028.

items.title: CMS: Starting July 1, 2026, Medicaid managed care contract and rate submissions must use the MC-Review state portal
items.summary: In a March 2026 informational bulletin, CMS reminded states that beginning July 1, 2026, all Medicaid managed care contract and rate submissions transmitted after that date must use the MC-Review state portal (submissions before July 1, 2026 can still be sent via email). The bulletin also notes states should begin reporting SDP-specific T-MSIS fields as soon as possible, but no later than September 2026.

items.title: SSA announces 2.8% COLA for 2026; new benefit amounts payable January 2026 (SSI starts Dec. 31, 2025)
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026. SSA says increased Social Security benefits are payable beginning in January 2026, and SSI payments reflecting the COLA begin on Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: IRS: 2026 EITC maximum credit is $8,231 for taxpayers with 3+ qualifying children
items.summary: The IRS published inflation adjustments for tax year 2026 and stated the maximum Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) amount is $8,231 for qualifying taxpayers with three or more qualifying children (up from $8,046 for 2025). The IRS directs filers to Revenue Procedure 2025-32 for the full EITC table, including thresholds and phase-outs.

items.title: USDA: WIC state agencies must allow at least one non-fresh fruit/vegetable option (frozen/canned/dried) by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS said recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026. FNS reported that as of November 2025, 95% of state agencies were already in compliance.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2026–27 Pell Grant maximum remains $7,395; minimum is $740 (effective July 1, 2026–June 30, 2027)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid issued a Dear Colleague Letter stating that for the 2026–27 award year (July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027), the maximum Pell Grant award remains fixed at $7,395 under continuing appropriations. The minimum Pell Grant award is 10% of the maximum, or $740 (after rounding rules).

items.title: Federal Student Aid: 2025–26 FAFSA submission deadline is June 30, 2026
items.summary: The 2025–26 FAFSA form can be submitted up through June 30, 2026 (for the 2025–26 award year). Students should confirm school and state deadlines may be earlier, but the federal processing deadline is June 30, 2026.

items.title: Federal Student Aid: Apply by April 23, 2026 for Title III designation and waiver of FWS/FSEOG non-federal share requirement
items.summary: Federal Student Aid reminded institutions to apply by April 23, 2026 for designation as a Title III institution and to request a waiver of the non-federal share requirement for Federal Work-Study (FWS) and Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG). Eligible institutions seeking the waiver must complete the application process by the April 23 deadline.

items.title: Pennsylvania LIHEAP deadline extended to May 8, 2026 for 2025–26 season (cash grants $200–$1,000; crisis grants up to $1,000)
items.summary: Legal Aid of Southeastern Pennsylvania reported Pennsylvania's 2025–26 LIHEAP season is open Dec. 3, 2025 through May 8, 2026 after an extension. The update notes cash grants of $200–$1,000 to help pay heating bills and crisis grants of $25–$1,000 for emergencies.

items.title: KFF: In FY2026, 41 of 48 responding states reported implementing Medicaid fee-for-service rate increases; 36 states planned benefit enhancements
items.summary: KFF’s annual Medicaid budget survey overview reported that three-quarters of responding states (41 of 48) implemented fee-for-service provider rate increases for at least one provider category in FY2026, while 6 states reported rate restrictions. KFF also reported 36 states planned to add or enhance benefits in FY2026, while 4 reported benefit cuts or limitations.
